hard.owner_title: Vice President, Asia Pacific, Japan & ME
hard.next_step: Confidence: 25% | 26 May: Technical team are leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack. Pushing for F2F when next in Manila; no decision or award likely until (our) Q3 | 11 May: No responses | 30 Mar: Good discussion at the RT event 26 Mar; on track for the 6 Apr demo | 13 Mar: Online demo scheduled 6 Apr; attending RT 26 Mar
hard.ais_why: Product scope is iContract (CLM) only, a single-module evaluation with no Merlin / Agentic AI / ANA SKU; the two dated NextStep entries (Mar-May 2026) cover RT participation and demo scheduling with zero AI, Merlin or agentic mention. AI is not a relevant evaluation dimension on this deal, so a score of 2 (Negative) reflects a focused contract-management-only opportunity, not weak fit.

north_star_verdict.headline: The deal has been dark for 73 days since the 24 Apr CLM demo and ADB's technical team is openly leaning to Coupa to complement its P2P stack; no champion, economic buyer, or value case exists and any decision is deferred to the buyer's Q3, past the 30 Oct close.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 116 (close 30 Oct 2026). Last verified Salesforce activity: 24 Apr 2026 (73 days ago). Time in Qualified stage: 136 days (qualified 20 Feb 2026). The 11 May NextStep recorded 'No responses' and the 26 May note put any decision in the buyer's Q3 (Jul-Sep). Buyer silence beyond 60 days forces Off Track regardless of stage, and the deferred Q3 decision makes the 30 Oct date non-credible on current pace.
evidence: Last buyer touchpoint: 24 Apr 2026 (73 days ago), a CLM demonstration; no buyer-facing deliverable executed since.
NextStep 26 May 2026 (rep-side note): 'Technical team are leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack. Pushing for F2F when next in Manila; no decision or award likely until (our) Q3'.
NextStep 11 May 2026: 'No responses' from the buyer.
Stage Qualified, amount $150k, with no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion identified across seven touchpoints Feb-Apr 2026 (only an Evaluator and an Influencer, both non-responsive).

Trajectory: holding steady vs the prior sweep (Off Track then, Off Track now) and if anything weaker, with the buyer now 10 days more silent; the single biggest blocker to a close is that Coupa is the technical team's default and Zycus has no internal voice or value case to counter it.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case has been captured for ADB's CLM evaluation across the seven touchpoints Feb-Apr 2026, and the Salesforce value/pain fields are unpopulated. The NextStep log and email trail cover only demo scheduling and the Coupa preference, never a cost, contract-volume, compliance-risk, or cycle-time figure the buyer stated. Without a financial anchor ADB has no basis to justify Zycus over Coupa's bundled P2P-plus-CLM. The move is to co-build a contract-management savings model tied to ADB's contract volume and compliance exposure and put it in front of procurement and finance before the Q3 decision.
sources: Avoma calls Feb-Apr 2026 (no pain/metrics captured; only the 17 Feb intro call was transcribed and carried no content)
NextStep__c 26 May / 11 May 2026 (scheduling and competitor notes, no value case)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in any Salesforce field, contact role, or captured call. The two confirmed buyer-side contacts are Anna Lee (Evaluator, Board Member of the ADB International Women's Committee) and Kenneith Pasigpasigan (Associate IT Officer, Project Procurement, Influencer), neither of whom controls budget, and both have been silent since 24 Apr 2026. The 26 May note references a 'technical team' leaning to Coupa but names no budget owner, and Carl Kimball is 'pushing for F2F when next in Manila' precisely because the power line is unmapped. The move is to use that Manila face-to-face to identify who owns the CLM budget within ADB's procurement leadership and to get an introduction from Kenneith Pasigpasigan.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Anna Lee = Evaluator, Kenneith Pasigpasigan = Influencer; no Economic Buyer role)

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's evaluation criteria are undocumented beyond the implicit signal that P2P ecosystem fit matters: the 26 May note shows the technical team values Coupa because it complements their existing P2P stack. No use-case review, RFP round, or business-requirement document has surfaced in any call or Salesforce field, so it is unknown what CLM capabilities ADB will weigh. The one criterion evidence supports is integration/ecosystem fit, on which Zycus is currently exposed as a CLM point solution. The move is to run a scoping conversation that surfaces ADB's actual CLM requirements (compliance, contract repository, cycle time, risk) and reframes the decision around CLM depth rather than suite bundling.
sources: NextStep__c 26 May 2026 ('to compliment their P2P stack')

decision_process.narrative: ADB's decision process is unmapped. The call sequence shows only Zycus-initiated demo scheduling (RT event 26 Mar, demo 6 Apr, rescheduled placeholders 14-24 Apr) coordinated through an IT assistant in the Finance and User Services Division, with no buyer-side owner driving toward a decision. The 26 May note states no decision or award is likely until the buyer's Q3, indicating ADB has not committed to a formal evaluation timeline. As a multilateral development bank ADB will carry complex, multi-approval procurement rules that have not been surfaced. The move is to ask directly, at the Manila face-to-face, what ADB's CLM selection and approval process is and when it runs.
sources: Salesforce activities Mar-Apr 2026 (demo scheduling emails via ADB ITFS)
NextStep__c 26 May 2026 ('no decision or award likely until (our) Q3')

paper_process.narrative: The contracting path is entirely unknown: no MSA, order form, SOW, legal review, or InfoSec step has been mentioned in any call, NextStep entry, or Salesforce field, which is expected for a Qualified-stage deal that has not yet reached commercials. ADB is a multilateral development bank likely to require formal tender, legal review, and multi-party approvals for an iContract CLM purchase. The single-module iContract scope would contract as an Order Form plus SOW, not a full-suite MSA. Visibility here only becomes relevant once ADB re-engages and a value case earns a commercial conversation.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by ADB. Across seven touchpoints the only substantive signal is competitive (technical team preference for Coupa's P2P fit), not a stated contract-management problem, pain owner, or compelling event. Inferred pain, given the iContract scope, is manual or fragmented contract lifecycle management (repository, compliance, renewals), but no ADB stakeholder has confirmed it. Without named pain the deal has no urgency and no internal owner, which is why it has stalled. The move is to re-engage Anna Lee or Kenneith Pasigpasigan and ask what contract-management problem is driving the evaluation, who owns it, and what happens if it is not solved.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Anna Lee (Evaluator) and Kenneith Pasigpasigan (Influencer) are the only confirmed buyer contacts, and neither has shown advocacy, access to power, or engagement since 24 Apr 2026; the 11 May note explicitly records 'No responses'. The 26 May reference to a 'technical team leaning to Coupa' shows there is an internal voice, but it is working against Zycus, not for it. With no advocate, Coupa's ecosystem-fit narrative goes unchallenged inside ADB. The move is to re-open the relationship with Kenneith Pasigpasigan, arm him with a CLM differentiation and savings story, and convert him or a procurement-leadership contact into an active champion.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Anna Lee, Kenneith Pasigpasigan; no Champion role)
NextStep__c 26 May 2026 ('technical team leaning to Coupa')

competition.narrative: Coupa is the sole named competitor and the current front-runner: the 26 May 2026 rep note states ADB's 'technical team are leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack', a clear ecosystem-fit advantage against a Zycus point-CLM play. No other competitor is named across any call, field, or NextStep entry, so this reads as Zycus iContract versus Coupa's bundled CLM, with Coupa holding the default position. Zycus's wedge is depth: iContract's contract compliance, repository, and risk-identification capability versus Coupa's shallower CLM bolt-on. That wedge is entirely unactivated (no champion, no value case, no procurement-leadership briefing), which is why Coupa is winning by inertia. The move is to get a differentiated CLM value case in front of ADB's budget owner before their Q3 decision.
sources: NextStep__c 26 May 2026 ('Technical team are leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack')
NextStep__c 26 May 2026 ('Pushing for F2F when next in Manila')
Salesforce Competitors__c not populated (competitor sourced from NextStep)

deal_movement.summary: The deal peaked early and has since deflated: the envelope was cut from $1M to $150k (single iContract module) on 13 Apr, ownership moved to VP Carl Kimball on 28 Apr, the close date was pushed 30 Sep to 30 Oct on 11 May, and by 26 May the technical team was leaning to Coupa with no buyer response since the 24 Apr demo.

items.change: Amount cut from $1,000,000 to $150,000 (scope narrowed to iContract CLM only)
Owner reassigned to Carl Kimball (VP APAC/Japan/ME)
Close date pushed from 30 Sep 2026 to 30 Oct 2026
Technical team recorded leaning to Coupa; decision deferred to buyer Q3

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the single strongest and only current threat: as of 26 May 2026 ADB's technical team is leaning to Coupa to complement their existing P2P stack, giving Coupa a live ecosystem-fit advantage over a Zycus CLM point solution. No other competitor has been named in any source Feb-May 2026, so the field is a two-way contest with Coupa as the default. Zycus is at real risk of losing by inertia unless it activates a CLM-depth value case with ADB's budget owner before the deferred Q3 decision.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Technical team are leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack
competitors.how_we_win: Counter the P2P-bundling story with iContract's CLM depth (contract compliance, repository, risk identification, obligation management) that a P2P-suite CLM bolt-on cannot match; quantify the cost of shallow CLM and put the case in front of ADB's procurement budget owner before their Q3 decision.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The one criterion ADB has signalled is P2P ecosystem fit, and on that dimension Zycus is exposed as a CLM-only vendor against Coupa's integrated suite; ADB's actual CLM requirements are undocumented.

best_practice_check.summary: This is an early, dark, single-threaded deal losing to Coupa by default; the win levers are re-engagement, a CLM-depth counter-narrative, and multi-threading to a budget owner.
flags: Counter Coupa's P2P-fit story now: lead with iContract's contract-compliance and risk depth that a bundled P2P-suite CLM cannot match, the only wedge on this deal.
Single-threaded to two non-responsive IT/evaluator contacts; multi-thread into ADB procurement leadership and finance to find a budget owner and a champion.
No value case after seven touchpoints; a quantified CLM savings model is the fastest way to re-earn a seat before ADB's Q3 decision.
